Package algorithm.gaaco

Class PheromoneMatrix

java.lang.Object
algorithm.gaaco.PheromoneMatrix
public final class PheromoneMatrix extends Object
Encapsulates the pheromone trail matrix used by the Ant Colony Optimisation stage.

Matrix layout

matrix[i][j] holds the pheromone level on the directed edge from node i to node j, where i and j are zero-based indices into the node list supplied at construction time. The diagonal is always 0.0 — a node cannot travel to itself.

Pheromone update rules

  • Evaporation — evaporateAll(double) multiplies every off-diagonal entry by (1 − ρ), uniformly decaying the trail on all edges, including those not visited in the current round. This corrects the original code's bug where unvisited edges were never decayed.
  • Deposit — deposit(int, int, double) adds a positive delta to a single directed edge. The ACO class calls this per-edge after a complete ant tour, using Q / tourLength as the delta. Using tour-total length (not cumulative in-step distance) matches the standard Ant System formula.

Thread safety

This class is not thread-safe, and it does not need to be. In the concurrent ACO design the live PheromoneMatrix is accessed only by the master thread: ant-1 deposits the GA route, and the master thread applies the batch evaporate-then-deposit after collecting all ant results. Concurrent ant workers (ants 2..N) receive a read-only double[][] snapshot via snapshot() and never touch this object directly. (The legacy code used AtomicDouble to serialise concurrent ant updates, which introduced a read-modify-write race anyway.)

  • Constructor Details

    • PheromoneMatrix

      public PheromoneMatrix(int nodeCount, double initialValue)
      Constructs a fresh pheromone matrix for nodeCount nodes.

      Every off-diagonal entry is initialised to initialValue; the diagonal is set to 0.0.

      Parameters:
      nodeCount - number of nodes (matrix dimension); must be ≥ 1
      initialValue - uniform initial pheromone level τ₀; must be > 0
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if any constraint is violated

  • Method Details

    • get

      public double get(int from, int to)
      Returns the pheromone level on the edge from node from to node to.
      Parameters:
      from - source node index [0, n)
      to - destination node index [0, n)
      Returns:
      pheromone level (≥ 0)

    • deposit

      public void deposit(int from, int to, double delta)
      Adds delta pheromone to the edge from from to to.

      The deposit amount should be Q / tourLength per the standard Ant System update rule.

      Parameters:
      from - source node index [0, n)
      to - destination node index [0, n)
      delta - pheromone amount to deposit; must be ≥ 0

    • evaporateAll

      public void evaporateAll(double rho)
      Applies global evaporation to every off-diagonal entry. 
        τ_ij ← (1 − ρ) · τ_ij   for all i ≠ j

      Calling this before depositing pheromone for a completed ant tour implements the standard Ant System update sequence and ensures that unvisited edges decay over time rather than retaining their initial level indefinitely.

      Parameters:
      rho - evaporation coefficient ρ ∈ [0, 1]

    • reset

      public void reset(double initialValue)
      Resets every off-diagonal entry to initialValue and every diagonal entry to 0.0.

      Call this to start a fresh ACO run without allocating a new matrix.

      Parameters:
      initialValue - new uniform pheromone level; must be > 0

    • snapshot

      public double[][] snapshot()
      Returns a defensive copy of the full pheromone matrix.

      The returned array is completely independent of this object: writes to the copy do not affect the live matrix, and subsequent calls to deposit(int, int, double) or evaporateAll(double) on this object do not affect the copy.

      Intended for use by AntColonyOptimization to hand a read-only pheromone view to each concurrent ant worker. Because ants only read from the returned array, no synchronisation is required during tour construction.

      Returns:
      n×n copy of the current pheromone levels

    • size

      public int size()
      Returns the number of nodes (the matrix dimension).
      Returns:
      node count
